title: Borana calendar
body: The Borana calendar is a calendrical system used by the Borana Oromo, a people living in southern Ethiopia and northern Kenya. The calendar was reported to have been developed around 300 BC found at Namoratunga. However, reconsideration of the Namoratunga site led astronomer and archaeologist Clive Ruggles to question the relationship between the calendar and the Namaratunga megaliths.  The Borana calendar consist of 29.5 days and 12 months for a total 354 days in a year. The calendar has no weeks but name for each day of the month. It is a lunar-stellar calendar system.
